question: Will the first eaglet hatch on April 11, 2026?

fragility_points: Missing direct access to the nest-specific 2026 lay-date log.
A current observed pipping/full-emergence sequence could tighten timing materially.
key_assumptions: Ordinary bald eagle incubation timing remains the dominant mechanism.
No outage or source-of-truth quirk materially distorts the visible hatch date.
Nest-specific lay-date evidence does not justify near-certainty on April 11.
main_logical_chain: Use contract text to determine what counts: first fully emerged eaglet visible on April 11 ET via the designated livestreams.
Anchor to species-level incubation base rates rather than narrative certainty.
Note that exact-date and livestream-resolution mechanics widen uncertainty relative to a generic hatch-window claim.
Conclude April 11 is likely but materially below the market-implied 94.45%.
payload.main_thesis: April 11 is plausible but the market is too confident on an exact-date wildlife outcome governed by visible full emergence and livestream timing.
quote_anchors: Hatch is defined as the first moment an eaglet is visibly fully emerged from its shell.
payload.source_quality_view: Good enough for a medium-difficulty outside-view memo: strong contract source, decent nest-specific context, and one independent biological source; the main gap is direct access to lay-date records.
strongest_disconfirmers: If the livecam/log-book community has a precise first-lay date that maps tightly to April 11, the market could be justified at a much higher probability.
strongest_supports: Cornell lists bald eagle incubation at 34-36 days, supporting April 11 as plausible but not uniquely certain.
Livecam operator description places hatching in March-April and says the pair raised three eaglets in 2024.
Exact-date contract wording leaves real probability mass on adjacent dates and visible-threshold timing noise.
payload.timing_relevance: This is an exact-date ET market with a visible-full-emergence rule and outage-return fallback, so small timing details matter disproportionately.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact 2026 first egg lay date for this nest.
How much informed livecam-community tracking is already embedded in market price.
payload.what_would_change_view: A credible nest-specific log or current livecam evidence showing the first egg date or imminent full emergence maps much more tightly to April 11 than the outside view assumes.

fragility_points: Lack of independently audited nest chronology in this run.
Near-midnight ET boundary between partial and full emergence.
Operational dependence on both named livestreams.
key_assumptions: The extreme April 11 price reflects real nest-tracking information rather than only crowd consensus.
Any visible progress reaches full emergence on April 11 ET rather than stopping at pip or partial emergence.
Both streams remain available enough that outage mechanics do not shift the recorded date.
main_logical_chain: The market is heavily concentrated on April 11, suggesting traders see a narrow hatch window.
The contract only counts first full visible emergence on the named livestreams in ET.
That keeps April 11 as the modal date but leaves nontrivial timing/verification slippage risk, so own probability should sit below market.
payload.main_thesis: April 11 remains the most likely resolution date, but the market is a bit too confident because full visible emergence, ET date boundaries, and rare dual-stream outage mechanics still leave meaningful slippage risk.
quote_anchors: Hatch is defined as the first moment an eaglet is visibly fully emerged from its shell.
If both livestreams are unavailable ... this market will resolve to the calendar date (ET) on which the livestream returns.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary mechanics are clear, but evidence independence is only low-medium and direct auditable support for the exact date remains thinner than the market price implies.
strongest_disconfirmers: No strong independent nest-specific incubation chronology was recovered in this run.
Pips or partial emergence on April 11 would not qualify under the contract.
A dual-stream outage near hatch time could shift the recorded date under the outage clause.
strongest_supports: Market price of 0.9445 implies traders strongly expect April 11.
Contract makes the decisive catalyst a concrete visible full-emergence event on named streams.
Both named YouTube source endpoints were verified as active/reachable in the extra verification pass.
payload.timing_relevance: Highest-information catalyst is the first stream-visible full emergence; if April 11 only gets partial progress or no clean visibility, repricing toward April 12 is the most plausible path.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact incubation timing for the first egg.
Whether any late-stage April 11 event would be pip-only or full emergence.
How outage-return evidence would present if both streams briefly failed.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would lower April 11 materially on direct chronology pointing later, April 11 partial-only emergence, or a dual-stream outage that could shift the recorded date.

fragility_points: Highlight metadata may omit a brief decisive event.
Market-side interpretation remains ambiguous without a verified live orderbook snapshot.
key_assumptions: The assignment current_price field is stale, side-inverted, or otherwise not a clean live YES quote.
Operator-posted highlight metadata would likely mention a decisive Apr 11 first full hatch if it occurred.
Cornell's 34-36 day incubation window explains why Apr 11 could have been the ex ante focal date.
main_logical_chain: Start with the extreme market prior and ask what would justify it.
Use contract wording to identify the exact qualifying event: first visibly fully emerged eaglet on Apr 11 ET via the named livestreams.
Check operator-posted Apr 8-11 materials for whether a decisive hatch had already happened.
Find that the nest is still framed as pre-hatch on Apr 8-9 and that Apr 11 recap metadata does not advertise a hatch.
Conclude the efficient present-tense view is strongly against Apr 11 as the realized first-hatch date.
payload.main_thesis: Apr 11 was a plausible expected hatch date ex ante, but by Apr 13 the public source-of-truth-adjacent evidence supports a non-Apr-11 first full hatch; any literal 94.45% YES read is likely stale or misinterpreted market data.
quote_anchors: Today’s highlights: Harry and Harriet arrive, then Harry and Harriet depart
payload.source_quality_view: Medium overall: the governing source is clear, operator materials are close to source-of-truth, Cornell is strong independent context, but no direct frame-level stream audit was done.
strongest_disconfirmers: No frame-level audit of the raw livestream timestamps was performed.
The 0.9445 assignment price could reflect side semantics or market-state details not recovered in this run.
strongest_supports: Contract wording makes the livestream timestamps the governing source and excludes pip/partial emergence.
Apr 8 and Apr 9 operator highlights still describe pip watch / steady incubation / final stretch before hatching.
Apr 11 retrospective highlight posted Apr 13 does not mention a hatch.
payload.timing_relevance: Critical: the contract is ET date-specific, Apr 11 had already passed by the Apr 13 run, and delayed highlight posts were used as the extra verification pass.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether current_price=0.9445 refers to YES, NO, or a stale/resolved representation.
Exact frame/timestamp of the first fully emerged eaglet was not independently recovered.
payload.what_would_change_view: A direct Apr 11 ET timestamped clip or operator statement showing first full emergence, or confirmation that the price field referred to a different side/state, would materially change the view.

fragility_points: Pip-to-full-emergence timing may straddle midnight ET.
Exact nest-specific egg chronology was not independently recovered in this run.
Dual-stream uptime and timestamp behavior are not fully audited.
key_assumptions: Nest-specific chronology is close enough to generic 34-36 day incubation timing that April 11 remains the modal day.
The gap between pip and visible full emergence does not shift the first qualifying hatch to an adjacent ET date.
Both designated livestreams remain usable enough that outage fallback rules do not determine the resolution date.
main_logical_chain: The market implies April 11 is almost certain at 94.45%.
Biological context supports April 11 as a plausible modal date but only within a range, not as a lock.
Because the contract resolves on visible full emergence under ET timestamps and outage fallback rules, adjacent-date and operational tails remain material.
Therefore April 11 is favored but overpriced relative to the true contract-resolving probability.
payload.main_thesis: April 11 is still the likeliest single resolution date, but the market is overconfident because full-emergence timing, ET date boundaries, and dual-stream outage rules leave more adjacent-date risk than a 94.45% price implies.
quote_anchors: Hatch is defined as the first moment an eaglet is visibly fully emerged from its shell.
If both livestreams are unavailable... this market will resolve to the calendar date (ET) on which the livestream returns.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary contract evidence is strong, contextual biology evidence is credible and independent, but nest-specific chronology remains incomplete, so confidence should not be extreme.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract counts only visible full emergence, not pip or partial emergence, so a biologically plausible overnight crossover can shift the resolution date.
If both streams are unavailable and later return, the market resolves to the ET date of return rather than the unseen physical hatch date.
Generic incubation references support a range, not near-certainty on one exact day.
strongest_supports: Independent bald eagle references cluster around 34-36 days or about 35 days of incubation, supporting a narrow expected hatch window.
Market consensus is strongly concentrated on April 11, suggesting nest-specific chronology likely points there.
Both designated YouTube pages are active live streams from the Great Lakes Bald Eagle Cam channel.
payload.timing_relevance: This is a date-sensitive market where the ET calendar date of visible full emergence, not the broader biological process, determines resolution.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether a reliable nest log exists that sharply narrows the first full-emergence date.
How much timestamp latency or operational ambiguity could matter at the exact hatch moment.
payload.what_would_change_view: Reliable nest-specific chronology tightly pinning first full emergence to April 11 would move me toward the market; evidence for April 10/12 timing, late April 10 pip activity, or stream instability would move me further away.
